title: AMA: Black seed oil, polyester clothes, sugar cravings, constipation, and detoxing from seed oils

chunks.title: Introduction and Personal Experience with Supplements
chunks.summary: The speaker shares a personal story about their experience with bone marrow and liver supplements, highlighting their benefits during pregnancy. They express relief from previous tiredness, moodiness, and cravings after taking these supplements.
chunks.transcript: absolutely necessary check out this review on bone marrow and liver from reagan b i'm 17 weeks pregnant and i don't have any symptoms or nausea but before taking these supplements i felt so tired and moody and depressed which is super unlike me i heard about this product through a midwife and i've been taking them for about three weeks now holy crap i will never go back i felt the difference within a couple of days and i just wish i had had them sooner i can't say enough about how great these supplements are i have no cravings either well before all i wanted was a hot dog for every meal lol i highly recommend for everyone especially if you're pregnant but even if you are not so that is on bone marrow and liver from

chunks.title: Announcement of AMA Podcast Format
chunks.summary: The speaker announces their transition to a new location in Costa Rica and the format of the podcast, describing it as an 'Ask Me Anything' episode where various health-related questions will be addressed. They invite listeners to submit questions for future episodes.
chunks.transcript: to help you reclaim your birth rights radical health the amount of reviews we get like this is staggering it really makes me smile it makes me feel so good about the work that the team at heart and soil is doing and i'm so happy that we are creating a product that is the highest quality that we possibly can that is in glass the only product of its kind in glass and the best organ supplements on the market grass-fed grass finish from new zealand do not settle for quality that is not new zealand if it doesn't say new zealand on the bottle it's not the same quality as hard and soil which is the good stuff heart and soil dot co reclaim your birthright radical health my people on this week's podcast i wanted to do an ama i'm in my new location guys i moved in costa rica and in my new house which is amazing i'm looking at the ocean and jungle right now for my window i've got my background i'm working on my new studio here but you're getting the studio version 1.0 i've got my banksy behind me there and my youtube award i'm going to get some diplomas on the wall um so you guys believe that i'm actually a real doctor that's a joke but i am um but in this week's podcast i'm going to do an ama and ask me anything podcast i'm going to cover black seed oil whether it's or not i'm going to cover uh how to cure constipation carbohydrate cravings what's to tell your family if they say you're crazy what about polyester underwear is that a problem i think it might be it's crazy stuff i'm going to go into tons of interesting things in this podcast in this ama there's a fun one to do i'll do more of these in the future i really enjoy this so sit tight enjoy the ama podcast if you want to sign up for my newsletter go to carnivoremd.com we'll have a new website up very soon we've got a whole re-brand coming but if

chunks.title: Effects of Polyester on Health
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the impact of polyester clothing on human health, citing studies on dogs that suggest polyester may negatively affect hormone levels and fertility. They advocate for transitioning to natural fabrics and share their personal experimentation with cotton clothing.
chunks.transcript: what is up true seekers welcome to another edition of the fundamental health podcast the location has changed but i am in an even better spot now in costa rica i wanted to do an ask me anything podcast this week to answer your burning questions in the past we have asked all of you through the carnivore md newsletter which you can sign up for at carnivoremd.com i've got a new website coming so stay tuned for that as well but in the previous emails we asked you guys what questions you have and so i have selected a variety of these for today's podcast these are always some of my favorite podcasts because i get to answer your questions and i hope it is valuable for you guys as well so in this episode i will cover polyester clothes do they affect your fertility i will cover black seed oil is it or is it amazing i will cover constipation how do you fix your constipation i will cover what to tell your family when they say you are killing yourself i'll talk a little bit about coronary artery disease i'll talk about ldl and whether you should worry about cholesterol i will talk about what to do for a sweet tooth or continued carbohydrate cravings on a carnivore or animal-based diet i will talk about if i ever fall off the wagon do i have any vices i will talk about how we speed up or how we might speed up detoxification of polyunsaturated fatty acids like omega-6 linoleic acid from cell membranes though we'll get into that one that's kind of a deep rabbit hole i will talk about why many quote unquote diet experts recommend vegetables for a healthy gut and what i think about this hint i'm not a fan i don't think you need vegetables for a healthy gut at all i will talk about whether the liver is a filter and whether you should fear it with toxins and much more so let's start with polyester clothing and whether this is a problem for humans or not i was originally skeptical i thought how can polyester be an issue for humans and to be fair there are no studies in humans but there are some very interesting studies in dogs which i will talk about in a moment which suggests that slinging your testicles or covering your lady parts in polyester clothing or underwear might not be a good thing for both male or female hormones and fertility i didn't believe it when i first saw it but it's interesting and it's compelling and it makes sense evolutionarily when we have had such synthetic fabrics next to our skin so increasingly i have been trying to eliminate all synthetic fabrics from my life to the point that i'm wearing only cotton shorts i'm in costa rica most of the time i don't have to wear underwear because what's the point of underwear when you're in costa rica and you just want to jump in the pool anyway and when i'm jumping in my pool i can wear cotton shorts which you guys have seen on my videos and you always ask what kind of shorts i wear if you guys really want i will build we will build a company that makes natural clothing natural swimwear natural loungewear natural shorts then i will recreate something like the shorts i have the shorts i have are just cotton there's nothing special about them but if you want something special we can do this but only if you guys enjoy this or you really something that you really are asking for but regardless in my own

chunks.title: Skin Care and Personal Hygiene
chunks.summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of not using topical products on the face and discusses their natural approach to skin care. They share insights on the necessity of certain fatty acids and how diet influences skin health.

groups was dressed in one type of textile underpants made from either 100 polyester 50 polyester 50 cotton 100 cotton or 100 wool the pants were worn for 12 months so these are female dogs dogs were electrostatic field that inhibits ovarian function however the effect proved to be reversible so i don't think we actually know what is going on there i don't know i don't think that polyester fabrics or polyester determinants or derivatives are moving across the skin but is this creating some sort of electrostatic potential that is changing the way the ovaries function it sounds so far-fetched but period the pants were worn so it wasn't heating the balls which we know is a bad thing for spermatogenesis by the end of 24 months there was a significant decrease in sperm count and modal sperm with an increase in abnormal forms the p value is less than point zero zero one wow okay testicular biopsy showed

chunks.title: Understanding an Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker simplifies what an animal-based diet entails, focusing on the importance of including meat, organs, and fruit. They explain the nutritional benefits and share anecdotes about conversations around dietary choices.
chunks.transcript: him what are you eating otherwise thinking i don't want people to just take our supplements at heart and soil i want people to actually change their diet and he just had a regular diet i said why don't you try something like an animal-based diet he said what's that i thought in my mind what is the simplest way that i can tell him what an animal-based diet is i said beef and fruit and he said i could do that every day of my life i love this so you all know that an animal-based diet is organs meat fruit honey raw dairy probably sunlight and connection with the ocean and grounding all those things as well but the simplest way to think of an animal-based diet is beef which is my favorite meat and fruit and i prefer beef because it's a ruminant because it eats grass and it's easier to get beef that is eating an evolutionarily appropriate diet than chicken and pigs and all those other things that are much harder to get that are actually eating what they should be eating so i thought you guys would find that quite interesting and that was my super simple appraisal of what an animal-based diet is beef and fruit throw on some organs you'll be great next question is from nick he says what

chunks.title: Addressing Concerns About Nutritional Advice
chunks.summary: Responding to listener concerns about cholesterol and dietary restrictions, the speaker shares views on cholesterol's role in the body and encourages followers to trust their own health experiences rather than mainstream advice.
chunks.transcript: what do i tell my friends and family who say that i am slowly killing myself second question from nick is is high cholesterol a problem to which i would answer no high cholesterol is not a problem and i have done many podcasts on lipids i will not go into tons of detail here except to say that on the cholesterol front cholesterol is a steroid molecule it is a steroid backbone molecule that is in every single cell membrane in your whole body it is transported by ldl low density lipoprotein essentially a bust full of cholesterol and triglycerides triglycerides are three-tailed fatty acids that can have either a polyunsaturated saturated or a monounsaturated fat as one of the three tails you will all remember from basic cell biology in high school hopefully that phospholipids are two-tailed fatty acid moieties or two-tailed moieties that form the cell membrane of your cells usually in a lipid bilayer a triglyceride is a three-tailed fatty acid molecule containing molecule that is transported in ldl ldl serves indispensable functions in your body without ldl you die yet mainstream medicine would tell you that it is harming you and i would say that is  if you are insulin sensitive if you have questions about how you know if you're insulin sensitive i've done many podcasts on that as well the very basic how to know if you're insulin sensitive is to get a fasting insulin which should be less than 5 micro iu per ml in further defense of the indispensable nature of ldl in the human body i want to show a couple of studies that illustrate how ldl acts as an immune molecule in the human body why would something that is an immune molecule be bad for us there must be more to the story april lipoprotein b which is the apolipoprotein contained on an ldl molecule as well as chylomicrons and others this molecule is an innate barrier against invasive staphylococcus aureus that is staph aureus that is one type of mrsa but you can also have methicillin sensitive or methicillin-resistant staph aureus regardless but able to protein b that is ldl is an innate barrier against invasive staph aureus infection okay therefore april lipper protein b levels at homeostatic levels in the blood are an essential innate defense against a factor an effector against invasive staph aureus infection the mechanism of antagonism entails binding of major structural protein of these lipoproteins a lipoprotein b to auto inducing to an autoinducing pheromone preventing its attachment to the bacteria and subsequent signaling through its receptor agrc so ldl and vldl lipid lipoproteins they are preventing quorum sensing this pheromonal quote unquote communication between bacteria ldl and vldl above protein b containing lipid proteins are essential in the immune response and yet they're killing us another study serum lipoproteins are critical for pulmonary innate defense against staphylococcus aureus quorum sensing very similar these are interventional studies together with previous reports demonstrating that hyperlipidemia also impairs lung innate defense these results suggest that maintenance of normal serum protein levels is necessary for optimal host innate defense in the lung what the authors point out at the beginning of this abstract is that hyperlipidemia probably connected with insulin resistance has been extensively studied in the context of atherosclerosis where the potential health consequences of the opposite extreme hypolipidemia remain largely uninvestigated circulating the proteins are essential carriers of insoluble lipid molecules and increasingly are recognized as innate immune effectors importantly severe hypolipidemia low ldl cholesterol which may occur with trauma or critical illness or if most cardiologists get a hold of you and put you on a statin is clinically associated with bacterial pneumonia now they just said trauma or critical illness in this abstract i editorialized statins and i think that if we do more research i have certainly observed this anecdotally and the people that i've worked with in my friends and my family and i believe there is research showing that lowering the ldl particles too low will be associated with increased incidence of bacterial pneumonia or other infections in humans these molecules ldl must be at an optimal level and that optimal level is has a wide range when you are insulin sensitive fasting insulin should be less than five but the key takeaway from that study was that hypolipidemia trauma critical illness also potentially from statins could predispose people to bacterial pneumonia and other infectious consequences because ldl has a valuable immune role in the human body yet we are told it is horrible for you is killing you how can it be doing both it is not doing both it is the context and circling back to nick's original question what do you say when your family says you are killing yourself you say to them well you honestly say to them i feel the best i have in my life or if that's true that's what you tell them you show them you don't tell them they will see you thriving and they will quickly understand there is more to learn because unless your family is a selection of preeminent physicians which you must debate and gather a huge amount of literature to discuss these issues with they are just repeating they are parroting regurgitating

chunks.title: Managing Carbohydrate Cravings
chunks.summary: The speaker provides strategies for overcoming carbohydrate cravings, emphasizing the importance of including organ meats and addressing mineral deficiencies. They also discuss the impact of caffeine on cravings.

transition and you'll be fine but i think the carbohydrate carvings go beyond that for most people and it probably is nutrient deficiency and or

chunks.title: Impact of Vegetables on Gut Health
chunks.summary: The speaker critiques the commonly held belief that vegetables are essential for gut health. They argue that fiber from plants may not contribute positively to gut diversity, encouraging listeners to rethink vegetable consumption.
chunks.transcript: question is from dominic who asks when was the last time you fell off the wagon what are your vices paul so i don't quote unquote fall off the wagon guys i think about things what are you eating he's not eating a lot of fat and he's not eating nearly enough fruit in his diet he's working out tons do not fear fruit do not fear fat add animal fats butter and tallow or ghee to your diet get fatty meats and add fruit and you will poop like beauty i trust trust me don't worry about that speaking of vegetables and pooping you don't need vegetables to poop this is a good time for harry's question which is why do so many so-called quote-unquote diet experts promote vegetables for a healthy gut do we need vegetables to have a healthy gut my answer is no absolutely not and i will show you literature that absolutely debunks that theory and i don't know why people continue to believe this is the case here is the framework for my answer to this question most people in the gut world who believe that they are gut experts or who think about the gut would look at a metric called alpha diversity which is a measure of the number of species within your gut as a measure of quote-unquote gut health there are epidemiology observational studies that show that in areas where people have more fiber they have better alpha diversity but that does not mean that more fiber from vegetables cause an increase in alpha diversity and as i will show you there are interventional studies where fiber is increased in someone's diet and there is no change in alpha diversity the gut doesn't get any healthier they did something else which did make the gut healthier which i'll tell you about in a moment and that can be a part of an animal-based diet but it is not vegetable fiber so all i can say is that the majority of these pundits who believe that fiber from plants is necessary for heavy gut are looking at epidemiology they're like an observational data because you look at interventional data and you do not see this there are interventional studies i have talked about in the past showing that adding fiber does not increase alpha diversity removing fiber does not decrease alpha diversity and there's another study that i'll show you right now interventional study another study showing adding fiber does not increase alpha diversity so all these people saying vegetables are necessary for healthy gut they're all wrong they're not reading literature they're parroting what everyone else says you don't want to be fiber fueled guys that's a horrible idea um and i think it's just gonna make you fart a lot so this is the study i was talking about from justin sonnenberg's lab would love to have a conversation with justin at some point but gut microbiotic

chunks.title: Detoxification from Seed Oils
chunks.summary: Discussing detoxification methods, the speaker warns against the negative effects of seed oils and polyunsaturated fatty acids. They recommend an animal-based diet as a way to support natural detoxification.
chunks.transcript: status this is in humans this is from stanford okay diet modulates the gut microbiome and got microbes in turn can impact the immune system i'm reading from the ad track so this is a 17 week randomized prospective study design combined with omics measurements right of the microbiome host including extensive immune profiling we found distinct effects of each diet they used two different diets they had plant-based fiber or fermented foods those were the two interventions high fiber consumers showed increased gut microbiome encoded glycan degrading caz enzymes despite stable community diversity there was no change in alpha diversity with increased fiber gut microbe microbiome encoded glycan degrading c z enzymes because you're giving your body more fiber of course it's going to have more fiber plant fiber degrading enzymes three distinct immunological trajectories in high fiber consumers correspond to baseline microbiota diversity alternatively the high fermented food diet steadily increased microbiota diversity and decreased inflammatory markers ah so they say our results indicate fermented foods may be valuable encountering the decreased microbiome diversity and increased inflammation pervasive in the industrialized society okay fair enough i'll agree with you there justin but you got to admit justin sonnenberg increasing the fiber did nothing for the microbial diversity or the inflammatory markers in the study why are people continuing to stay on the bandwagon of eat more fiber plant fiber is good that is complete but what is not  is fermented foods now first question why is there an inflammatory phenotype in the industrialized society i would say it's seed oils and lectins from your vegetables you can remove those that will remove inflammation from your gut as well if you guys have seen any of my stories on instagram you'll know i do fermented goat's milk kefir every single day or i'll do fermented cow's milk kefir every single day i'll do a video soon on instagram showing how i make that it's real simple you take milk you take kefir grains and you combine them you leave them outside in a glass jar with a top on it for a couple of days depending on the temperature it's warm here in costa rica within a few days i can get fermented milk there's less lactose in there it's raw goats milk or raw cow's milk and it's great if i drink goat's milk or cow's milk without fermenting it i will get stomach pains and gas because i am lactose intolerant but i ferment the goats and cow's milk so that is how i get fermented foods in my diet you can get fermented foods in your animal-based diet in a variety of ways if you don't want to do dairy you can make water kefir you can just take honey and mix it in water and you can put water kefir grains in there people say honey is anti-microbial it works fine you can ferment honey in a jar with water kefir grains some people will take processed sugar or brown sugar i would not recommend those i would take i've fermented coconut water in the past with kefir grains you can do all sorts of natural sugars with kefir grains either water or milk kefir grains and you can get some fermented food in your life on an animal-based diet i think it's great like i said i'll do a video showing that very clearly but there is no indication that fiber is beneficial for the gut in terms of inflammation or alpha diversity people are just wrong kelsey asks how to speed up detoxing from seed oils so if you followed any of my content you know i'm not a fan of seed oils corn canola sunflower safflower soybean peanut etc this is primarily because these are high in polyunsaturated fatty acids like the 18 carbon linoleic acid and what we know about humans and monogastric animals pigs and chickens and turkeys and ducks which is why i'm also not a fan of eating those animals if they are fed corn and soy because that will accumulate in their bodies as well is that none of those monogastric animals can get rid of polyunsaturated fatty acids through saturation so desaturation is the addition of a double bond to a molecule and saturation is the removal of a double bond saturated fats have no double bonds in them but humans do not have saturating enzymes for polyunsaturated fatty acids we can interconvert monounsaturated fatty acids and saturated fats in the human body but we can't interconvert poly and mono meaning if there is more than one double bond in your fatty acid molecule you're stuck with it which is why i don't think humans should be eating a lot of linoleic acid or polyunsaturated fatty acids in general we need a little bit and i think sure get some omega-3s dha epa dpa they occur in animal fat they occur in egg yolks that's all you need i'm not a fan of fish oil i've done previous videos on that it's rancid it's oxidized you don't need that much epa and dha in your body we know that too much epa and dha can imbalance things like clotting etc they can clearly get toxic and omega-3s absolutely absolutely but you can also clearly get toxic in things like linoleic acid which is essentially a hormone in the human body and is a fat soluble vitamin and i wouldn't even really call it a vitamin but it's a fat-soluble nutrient we need a small amount but like every other fat soluble vitamin vitamin a vitamin d vitamin e vitamin k you can get too much and it accumulates in your body think about it like that you don't want to get linoleic acid toxic you don't want to be toxic in this fat soluble quote unquote nutrient you need a small amount where do you get the small amount wild animals will give it to you in their fat in amounts of one to two percent of their fat maybe a little more than two percent seed oils 20 30 40 50 60 olive oil avocado oil 15 to 20 linoleic acid why i'm not a fan of those they're much better than seed oils still not a fan of them why are you not eating animal fats tallow and butter two to three percent linoleic acid so when that accumulates in our cell membrane as kelsey is asking how do we get rid of it we don't know we don't know there's maybe one study i've seen that shows that the half-life of those polyunsaturated fatty acids in your cell membranes is long the half-life is six months maybe more if you mean which means pharmacokinetically you need four and a half half-lives at least to get rid of all of it so you're talking about two and a half years to get rid of those polyunsaturated fatty acids now that's assuming you're a normal human who's probably in taking more polyunsaturated fatty acids but hypothetically could we possibly push some of those out if you're eating more animal fats it's reasonable so the only strategy that i think is viable what's reasonable is to limit your linoleic acid eat a low linoleic acid diet let your body get rid of that linoleic acid in your life i would actually say it's probably not even a low linoleic acid diet it's just a normal evolutionarily consistent linoleic acid diet amount in your life

chunks.title: Final Thoughts on Dietary Supplements
chunks.summary: The speaker provides a critical view of common dietary supplements, such as black seed oil and turmeric, discussing their potential side effects and positioning them as medicine rather than primary food sources.
